summary: eth-wallet-helpers ships a hidden base64 payload that is decoded, executed, and persisted on module load
details: index.js contains a top-level IIFE that, 37 seconds after require(), reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat, base64-decodes ~40KB of JavaScript, writes it to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0700, and spawns it detached under node. The payload file is disguised as a test fixture and is not referenced by any test. The same IIFE installs cross-platform persistence for the dropped script: a crontab entry running every 12 hours on Linux, a scheduled task named 'WinNodeSync' on Windows, and a LaunchAgent labelled com.apple.syncd (impersonating an Apple system daemon) under ~/Library/LaunchAgents on macOS. Any consumer that requires this package executes the opaque bundled payload and gains a scheduled re-execution foothold on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
